name: Bootstrap an elite autonomous development environment for
description:
Bootstrap an elite autonomous development environment for this workspace before building the actual hackathon project.

Primary goal:
Create a world-class autonomous software engineering environment optimized for winning the TestSprite Hackathon.

Phase 1 — Environment Bootstrap

Clone, study, and actively adopt concepts, workflows, and best practices from:

* https://github.com/obra/Superpowers
* https://github.com/anthropics/skills/tree/main/skills/frontend-design
* https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/tree/main/plugins/code-review
* https://github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem
* https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

For each repository:

1. Clone into ./vendor or ./tools.
2. Read all documentation and identify useful capabilities.
3. Produce an integration report explaining:

   * what was adopted
   * what was rejected
   * why
4. Apply the concepts actively, not passively.

Create and maintain these directories:

* Brain/
* Memory/
* vendor/
* skills/
* reviews/

Operational rules for the entire session:

* Read Brain/ before major decisions.
* Maintain persistent lessons in Memory/lessons_learned.md.
* Perform self-review before every commit.
* Never declare work complete without real verification.
* Use this mandatory loop:

Plan → Implement → Verify → Root Cause Analysis → Fix → Review → Verify Again

Frontend work must follow frontend-design principles.

Code review must be mandatory before merge or finalization.

Document architecture decisions continuously.

After bootstrap is complete:

1. Analyze multiple TestSprite hackathon ideas.
2. Select the highest expected-value idea.
3. Design architecture.
4. Build the project autonomously with minimal human interruption.
5. Maintain LOOP.md automatically during all iterations.

Minimize questions to the human unless completely blocked.
